title: Rolling Shutter
description: The rolling-shutter (CMOS "jello") camera artifact as a live filter: a sensor reads out one scanline at a time from top to bottom, so each row of the image is sampled a moment later than the one above it. Pan or shake fast and verticals skew, the whole frame wobbles like jelly, and a bright flash paints only a band of the picture. Readout direction and speed, skew, jello wobble, motion smear and a partial-exposure flash band are live and mappable — works on a camera, a dropped photo/video, the demo, or the layers below.
